item.title: I've been lying to everyone since Saturday...
item.description:
This is going to be long, because I just need to get it all out. About a year ago, after being a member of the local art gallery for a few months, I took a chance and applied for my own exhibit, and was awarded one for December/January of 2024/2025. I couldn't believe it, and was quite excited at first. My medium is scale miniatures. I fully admit that when compared to other mediums, it's not nearly as impressive. I also know that when it comes to this medium, there are much more proficient and accomplished artists (I've posted some of my work on Imgur). My goal, however, was to attempt to show that scale models are a legitimate medium in an artistic sense. So for the past year I've been building things and deciding what I would put in the gallery. They have a limited number of pedestals to display three dimensional art, like pottery and sculpture, so I limited myself to 10 pieces, 2 of which didn't require pedestals. I felt this was sufficient and a good representation of my work. The event space would be shared with another artist. He paints with acrylic and oils mainly, and his work is AMAZING. Most of it is abstract but involves anything from pop culture references to landscapes, psychedelic patterns and religeous iconography. When we brought our work in a couple weeks before the opening, I was awe struck by how good his work was. I also realized that quanity wise, I was lacking. He had over 50 pieces to show. They were different sizes, shapes, themes, and subjects. The variety and quality was amazing. The contrast between his work and mine, made me feel... rather inadequate. But I told myself they were different mediums. I was still proud of my work. So I pushed that animal back down and kept it from consuming me. I did what I could to help, but the gallery is set up by the director. She does a great job, and takes the full two weeks to use the work we submit to fill the space so it works together. We can give input if we want, but I know as a trained and educated artist, she knows much more about this stuff than I do. I just glue things together and paint them. So I left it up to her.
I stopped in on Friday before the exhibition, and the impact of how limited my work was began to hit me more. My work seemed to almost disappear in the vast sea of the other artists bold and impressive work. I should have done more. I should have provided more of my work. I admit that I was limited by the number of display pedestals they had, but I could have pushed more to build my own pedestals to display my work, or some way to provide more room for my pieces. I did have a year. That's completely on me. Opening night came and at first, there were only a few people. Most of the members come to the openings, but many didn't for this one. It's the holiday season, so many people have other obligations. The other artist promoted his opening quite a bit on social media, with the help of his significant other. About a half-hour into it, people for his event began to file in. The room was full. Everyone was admiring his work and taking in the impact that it gave. I had provided small narratives of my work to describe how I got the idea, what I'd used to create it, or other information to help describe the piece. Since the work will be there until January, I wanted people to be able to read about the work without me present. For the opening, I'd prepared myself to talk to people who looked interested in my work, and answer any questions they might have. That proved to not be something I needed to do. Very few people seemed to be interested in my work. Many people used the pedestals to hold their drinks or food while they talked with others. I saw some children interested in a couple of my pieces, like the model train layout I had recently completed, but this only reinforced the idea in my head that my work is amateur, immature, and not a viable art medium. I attempted to talk to the other artist, but he was often busy talking to all the people who had shown up to see his work. The director did make a speech introducing us and describing our work. Obviously, he had much more to describe, and he was also personally known by many of the other artists in the gallery, since he was a tattoo artists as well, and had done personal work for them. Two people I knew showed up for my exhibit. I fully admit I didn't set up an event on social media like the other person had, but I'm also not great at self promotion. Three people asked me about my work, one of which was a child who kept asking me where I bought the models, and didn't seem to understand they didn't come painted or with the diorama already built. The next day, some people asked how my "thing" went. I told them it went fine. I haven't described it past that, because I simply cannot. I felt dismissed and unimportant on Saturday. I felt like the whole thing was a joke; a pity exhibit awarded to me because they needed floor space to even out the room. I left about 15 minutes before closing, because I was getting anxious about drinks and food being near my work, and seeing some of the pedestals wobbling after being bumped into, didn't want to witness seeing my work fall onto the floor. I don't think I would have reacted well. TL;DR - I put some of my scale models in an art gallery and it was generally ignored and dismissed. Thank you for letting me vent. Edit: I appreciate everyone's feedback and comments. I'm trying to read through all of them and reply or give answers if I can. But thank you for the support and kind words and above all else, your honesty. A link to my work: https://imgur.com/gallery/84VFcER
item.dc:creator: PleaseNotTheFace

item.title: Wearing Marcille's Hairstyles from Delicious in Dungeon for a week!!
item.description:
I recently watched the anime Delicious in Dungeon/Dungeon Meshi and I'm now OBSESSED with it. I knew from the first episode that I'd have to try out Marcille's hairstyle on my own hair, but then she KEPT CHANGING HER HAIRSTYLES?? What is this??
Like seriously I thought it would just be this hairstyle the whole time but she changed her hair like 30+ times throughout the first season ?
Most of the hairstyles were definitely improved by the addition of elf ears, but not all of them needed it!
This 2-1 hairstyle was probably my favorite from the week, I'm definitely wearing this and the crown braid variation again
Thanks for watching and I hope you enjoyed!! Have a great week, friends :)
item.dc:creator: GingerSnark

item.title: People are actually upset about Ciri?
item.description: Ciri being the new Witcher is nothing new. It's part of the books and was foreshadowed at the end of the third game. You being surprised by this, isn't a product of "wokeness", but rather, your lack of media literacy and/or straight up refusal to read.

item.title: Rest easy
item.description: My grandma died. Her name was Olivia. She was 77. She had an infectious laugh and smile. I love this photo of her. Last year I made a post about her telling me what she wanted to be buried in and now I get to make sure her wishes come true. Hug your loved ones.
item.dc:creator: vudumommajuju

item.title: If you know, you know. Welcome to my happy place.
item.description: This has been a long time coming. Eight years ago I lost my wife to cancer and since then my whole life has shifted. Nothing is the same, home, country, job, pretty much the only thing still with me is that toolbox. I've built this workshop myself, without any experience, just an idea, a goal and a whole lot of stubborness and perseverance. It's a bit rough round the edges but it's mine and it's hopefully the start of the next chapter of my life.
item.dc:creator: Uktech1

item.title: Also, caring about the opinions of people who spam such things is a form of self-harm.
item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: Update to yesterday
item.description: Yesterdays post: https://imgur.com/gallery/silver-linings-wjdIoSM Can't stand my family anymore. Christmas is always alcohol, fights and remarks about me. There has been more and more homophobia towards me lately so I just had to get out, decided to live in my car for the time being. I vented about it here yesterday, and people offered to help. I was definitely not expecting this support. I put up a Ko-fi page which many of you donated to, which reeeally helps me out with food and other things in the coming weeks. At first I was feeling very anxious about my decision to leave, especially with christmas coming up and I didn't really have much to begin with. Today however, thanks to you guys I really feel relieved and positive about this. I can't thank you enough for helping me. Thank you for supporting and making me feel like I am not alone. I may have found a place to stay over christmas, depending if the price is doable. If not I will still be pretty comfortable in my car thanks to all of you guys. I am really speechless over the huge support
item.dc:creator: akalse

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: Igurians- These donkeys need your help!
item.description: https://gofund.me/f071d5da On December 12th, 2 dogs crawled under a gate for a fenced in pasture associated with a Methodist Church that my mother attends in my small town. The 2 mini donkeys that spend time there were attacked for nearly an hour before anyone intervened. The male was torn to shreds. His jaw barely attached. Please help out if you can. The full story is on the gofundme. Thank you for viewing. Edit: These are therapy animals for children with mental health disorders. Edit: Link to local news story: https://www.whio.com/news/local/therapy-donkey-icu-after-being-attacked-by-dogs-greene-co/OAVEXYPJHFHTNLBR65FH6BSJOI/
item.dc:creator: Dokukaeru

item.title: A memedump to kickstart your week <3
item.description:
They called her "the incubator"
Take notes guys, he speaks the truth (and I lowkey feel called out? :'D)
He needs to calm down
Cursed tubbies in a found footage style? After the garfield horror stuff was so popular and they make a horror pac man game now, I wouldnt be surprised
Not again, brain
Don't judge, girls can be thirsty too
Oh lawd he comin'
I know some of y'all need to hear this: Collect your cups and move them to the kitchen! (me too..)
Yup, this person is my cat
Uhh excuse me? The vase literally broke on its own. Idk what you're talking about
Not pictured: Luigi becoming the new batman
Please? Universe?
Cat tax :) Have a good day!
